title: Platform Hold
prompt: First-person observational diary realism, already in motion at eye level beside the yellow tactile paving of an underground Montreal metro platform. A blue STM metro train with a white stripe is stopped along the platform, its doors closed, the cars subtly vibrating as if breathing in idle. The camera does not reset or pose; it begins with a slight handheld drift and micro sway, angled toward the train windows so passengers inside are visible making small natural gestures—someone seated looking down, another standing and shifting weight, quiet lived-in commuter behavior. Keep the STM logo and the French text 'MOUVEMENT COLLECTIF' readable on the train as continuity anchors. Fluorescent station light reflects softly on the train exterior, with muted overcast color and gentle contrast. Maintain coherent depth and realistic perspective from a person waiting close to the platform edge. Over the 4 seconds, let the gaze linger, then subtly begin a motivated turn away from the train toward the station interior, ending with lateral body motion that suggests the decision to leave the platform and naturally leads into the corridor and McGill sign shot.

title: Under the McGill Sign
prompt: Continue the same first-person camera journey mid-turn from the platform, entering a beige tiled McGill station passage at eye level with a gentle forward glide already underway. The dark blue 'McGILL' sign anchors the frame overhead while two illuminated fashion advertisements glow along the wall, their polished light contrasting with the matte beige tile floor and walls. Keep the motion natural and human, as if walking steadily after leaving the platform: slight handheld drift, soft footstep bob, no theatrical sweep. Pass beneath the sign and let the camera briefly settle just enough to register the station identity and shared public space, then tilt and drift toward the stairs with metal railings. The ads, tiled surfaces, and corridor geometry should stay coherent and realistic, preserving the underground atmosphere while the movement gradually commits upward. By the end of the 4 seconds, the frame should be oriented into the stair ascent, with rising energy and brighter light ahead so the next outdoor shot can begin as a direct continuation of the climb.

title: First Step Into Gray Light
prompt: Continue directly from the upward station movement into open air, as if stepping out of McGill and already walking forward at eye level along a narrow city sidewalk under gray overcast daylight. The camera is in gentle first-person motion from the first frame, with soft walking bob and realistic momentum. Keep the fenced construction area with red and white barriers close on one side, and older stone and brick facades on the other, including the lit Chicken Plus + sign with Korean and French text as a clear continuity anchor. Historic brick buildings with dormer windows rise above, adding texture and neighborhood specificity. A few pedestrians move naturally ahead or past the camera, maintaining ordinary city spacing and pace without acknowledging the lens. Preserve a grounded urban palette, slightly damp-looking light, and believable street depth. The shot should feel like a lived continuation of the commute, not a reveal: constrained sidewalk, imperfect surfaces, shared movement. End still moving forward toward a busier block so the next shot can open with the same walking rhythm and expanded street life.

title: Shared Block
prompt: Continue the same first-person walking rhythm into a busier sidewalk block, beginning already in forward drift at eye level as the narrow street opens into a more populated stretch. Keep the urban geography coherent and grounded: restaurant signs for Aunt Dai with phone number, Song Thè bubble tea, and Pole Bento should appear as readable continuity anchors along the storefronts, with a leafless tree near the sidewalk. People move in both directions with natural everyday timing—pedestrians passing, small groups talking, diners seated outside or near the frontage—creating a calm shared street rhythm. The camera remains modest and observational, not staged: slight handheld sway, slow forward drift, then a brief gentle settle to notice signs, faces, storefront details, and the tree before continuing just enough to let the block breathe. Preserve soft overcast light, muted city colors, and realistic urban ambience. The shot should conclude without a clean stop, holding a sense of continued life beyond frame, as if the walk and the day simply keep going.
